prevalence.trend: stable [due to improved awareness but consistent genetic predisposition]

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: IADVL clinical criteria, Koebner phenomenon, Auspitz sign

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% [moderate - limited by access in rural areas]

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: IADVL guidelines, clinical diagnosis standard, biopsy rarely needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical steroids, coal tar preparations, limited phototherapy (PUVA/NB-UVB in tertiary centers), systemic methotrexate, limited biologics (high cost), Ayurvedic options (e.g., Panchatikta ghrita, neem)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Very limited phototherapy access, expensive biologics, severe psychosocial stigma, dermatologist shortage in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic topicals and methotrexate, IADVL guidelines, Ayurvedic formulations, growing biosimilar market
